feed.title: Christopher Alberto — Blog
feed.subtitle: Notes on WordPress engineering, WooCommerce builds, and shipping for the modern web.

entry.title: Ditching the Drag-and-Drop: My Journey to Faster WordPress with FlyntWP
entry.summary: I've spent years building WordPress sites, and while page builders promised speed and ease, they often delivered bloat.

entry.title: Building Beyond the Builder: Why Custom WordPress Themes Win on Performance and Scale
entry.summary: For anyone diving into WordPress, the allure of page builders like Elementor or Divi is undeniable.

entry.title: From Bloatware to Blade: Why Sage is My WordPress Development Game-Changer
entry.summary: I've spent years navigating the WordPress ecosystem, and if there's one constant frustration I've encountered, it's the struggle between rapid development and…

entry.title: Building Better: Why I Chose FlyntWP for Lightning-Fast WordPress Development
entry.summary: I've built my fair share of WordPress sites over the years, and I’ve seen the rise and, frankly, the performance fall of many popular low-code page builders.

entry.title: Decoupling WordPress: Building a Headless Backend with CI/CD for Production
entry.summary: I've spent years pushing WordPress beyond its blog-centric origins, and lately, my focus has shifted dramatically towards leveraging it as a robust, headless…

entry.title: From Bloat to Brilliance: Unpacking the Power of Custom-Coded WordPress Themes
Why I Ditched Page Builders for Hand-Coded WordPress: The Performance & Scalability Edge
entry.summary: For years, like many of you, I dabbled extensively with WordPress page builders.

entry.title: Beyond the Block Editor: Architecting Headless WordPress with GitHub Actions
entry.summary: For years, WordPress has been the backbone of countless websites, renowned for its ease of use and vast plugin ecosystem.

entry.title: That Lighthouse Score and the Headless Rabbit Hole I Fell Down
entry.summary: I remember staring at the Lighthouse report for that client's old WordPress site last year.

entry.title: That Time I Rebuilt a Site After Its Page Builder Imploded
entry.summary: I remember this one project, maybe three years back now.

entry.title: Why I Finally Went Headless on My Last WordPress Project (And How I CI/CD'd It)
entry.summary: I remember sitting at my desk a few months back, staring at a particularly gnarly WordPress template file.

entry.title: Why Your WordPress Site is Slow: The Hidden Cost of All-in-One Themes and Page Builders
"Just Install a Plugin For That": My Take on When to Code It Yourself in WordPress
entry.summary: I’ve been building websites with WordPress for, well, longer than I care to admit sometimes.
